name: Right Sidebar
description: Right-hand sidebar implemented as one unit: multiple sections, each rendered as a visual block with its own elevation (box-shadow), stroke (border), and background. Gaps between sections. Full structure and styles are in the component JSON (structure, cssClassStyles).

usageGuidance.when: Application needs right-hand navigation or contextual panel. Implement as multiple section blocks (with gaps, elevation, stroke), not one giant strip. See component JSON for structure and cssClassStyles.
antiPatterns: Don't use multiple RightSidebar instances in the same layout
Don't implement as one giant sidebar - use the sections array: each section is { items: NavItem[] }; multiple sections = multiple grouped blocks.
Don't put primary navigation in right sidebar - use LeftSidebar for main nav

notes.variant: Match theme (cp, vp, ppm, maconomy) for correct styling
notes.sections: Each element of the sections array renders as one visual block (one right-sidebar__section div). Each block has: elevation (box-shadow), stroke (border, border-right none), background, border-radius. The nav has gap between section blocks (e.g. gap: var(--space-3) in component JSON cssClassStyles). They function as one unit (one nav, one component). API: each section is { items: NavItem[] }; no section title in the API. Full spec: component JSON structure (nav > section divs) and cssClassStyles (.right-sidebar, .right-sidebar__section).

examples.name: With sections
examples.description: Multiple visual blocks with gaps, each with elevation and stroke (see component JSON).
